core_question: Should we invest in or prepare for nuclear fusion as a commercially viable energy source within the next 15 years?
central_thesis: The storyline should be led by the strongest public evidence, then translated into management choices.
narrative_focus: Evidence is densest around: funding, timeline, adoption, technology
structure_logic: Open with the executive decision, show source-backed data exhibits, then move through business implications, risks and action gates.
evidence_must_cover: To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

All 8 awardees are presently working toward presenting pre-conceptual designs and technology roadmaps of their FPP concepts within the first 18 months of the Milestone program—roughly late 2025 (18 months into the Milestone Program).

Department of Energy (DOE) today announced $107 million in funding for six projects in the Fusion Innovative Research Engine (FIRE) Collaboratives , and that several privately funded fusion companies have completed early critical-path science and technology (S&T) milestones in the Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program (“the Milestone Program”).

hypotheses_to_test.hypothesis: Nuclear fusion will not achieve grid-connected electricity generation before 2040.
hypotheses_to_test.decision_relevance: If true, near-term investment in fusion is premature; focus on other low-carbon technologies.
needed_evidence: Published timelines from ITER, SPARC, and other major projects; historical delays in fusion research.

hypotheses_to_test.hypothesis: The LCOE of first-generation fusion plants will be higher than solar+storage by 2035.
hypotheses_to_test.decision_relevance: If true, fusion will struggle to compete without carbon pricing or subsidies.
needed_evidence: LCOE projections from fusion companies and independent analysts; solar+storage cost trends.

hypotheses_to_test.hypothesis: Private fusion funding has shifted from early-stage to later-stage rounds, indicating maturation.
hypotheses_to_test.decision_relevance: If true, fusion is attracting serious capital; if false, it remains speculative.
needed_evidence: Funding data by round size and stage; number of startups reaching Series C or beyond.

hypotheses_to_test.hypothesis: Tritium supply constraints will limit fusion deployment to <10 GW by 2050.
hypotheses_to_test.decision_relevance: If true, fusion scale-up is bottlenecked; alternative breeding strategies needed.
needed_evidence: Tritium production capacity from CANDU reactors and other sources; fusion reactor tritium consumption rates.

hypotheses_to_test.hypothesis: Regulatory frameworks for fusion will be less stringent than fission, reducing licensing time and cost.
hypotheses_to_test.decision_relevance: If true, fusion may have a deployment advantage over advanced fission.
needed_evidence: Comparison of regulatory approaches (e.g., US NRC, UK EA); licensing timelines for fusion vs. fission.

hypotheses_to_test.hypothesis: Industrial heat applications (e.g., steel, cement) will be the first commercial fusion market, not electricity.
hypotheses_to_test.decision_relevance: If true, fusion companies should target industrial customers; grid electricity may be secondary.
needed_evidence: Company announcements of industrial partnerships; heat demand and temperature requirements.

hypotheses_to_test.hypothesis: China will lead fusion commercialization due to state-backed projects and supply chain control.
hypotheses_to_test.decision_relevance: If true, Western investors may face competitive disadvantage; policy implications.
needed_evidence: China fusion budget and milestones; rare earth magnet supply chain; CFETR timeline.

market_sizing_logic.formula: Total addressable market (TWh) = Global electricity demand (TWh) * % addressable by fusion (based on grid parity and baseload needs)
known_limitations: Assumes fusion achieves cost parity; ignores regional variations

market_sizing_logic.formula: Market size (GW) = Number of fusion plants * average capacity per plant (GW)
variables: Number of plants by 2050 (based on announced projects and realistic build rates)
Average plant capacity (e.g., 500 MW for ARC-class)
known_limitations: Highly speculative; depends on technology success and regulatory approval

market_sizing_logic.formula: Adopted capacity (GW) = (Number of potential customers) * (adoption rate) * (average capacity per customer)
variables: Potential customers: utilities, industrial heat users, data centers
Adoption rate based on cost competitiveness and risk appetite
Capacity per customer (e.g., 100 MW for data center)
known_limitations: Adoption rate is highly uncertain; early adopters may be niche

market_sizing_logic.formula: Value pool ($) = (Electricity revenue) + (heat revenue) + (hydrogen production revenue) + (other byproducts)
known_limitations: Byproduct markets are nascent; revenue stacking uncertain

market_sizing_logic.formula: Maximum deployable capacity (GW) = (Tritium supply) / (tritium consumption per GW-year) * (breeding ratio factor)
variables: Tritium production from CANDU and other sources
known_limitations: Tritium breeding not yet demonstrated; supply constraints may be binding

charting_rule: Charts may use only evidence_ledger values, dates, source counts or same-unit comparable values tied to public URLs.
